SEARCH
Package io.github.elevateddev.lattice.routing

Class JoinSpec<O>

java.lang.Object
io.github.elevateddev.lattice.routing.JoinSpec<O>
Type Parameters:
O - joined output type
public final class JoinSpec<O> extends Object
Configures a join stage.

By default, joins use long stamps extracted from Stamped inputs. Use stampLong(ToLongFunction) for allocation-free primitive stamp extraction from custom message types, or stamp(Function) when a join key is not naturally represented as a long.

    Handling for duplicate values from the same branch and stamp.

    Handling for groups that cannot receive every expected branch.

  • Method Details

    • allOf

      public static <O> JoinSpec<O> allOf(Function<JoinGroup,? extends O> combiner)
      Emits when every input branch has provided the same stamp.

      The default stamp extractor expects Stamped inputs and uses their primitive long stamp.

    • anyOf

      public static <O> JoinSpec<O> anyOf(Function<JoinGroup,? extends O> combiner)
      Emits on the first input branch for each stamp.

      Later arrivals for that stamp are handled by the duplicate policy while the stamp remains open. Once every branch has been seen, the runtime releases the retained first-arrival value and forgets the stamp. The default stamp extractor expects Stamped inputs and uses their primitive long stamp.

    • stamp

      public JoinSpec<O> stamp(Function<Object,?> stampExtractor)
      Uses an object-valued join stamp.

      Prefer stampLong(ToLongFunction) for long-compatible stamps to keep the runtime join table on its allocation-free path.

    • stampLong

      public JoinSpec<O> stampLong(ToLongFunction<Object> stampExtractor)
      Uses a primitive long join stamp.

      This is the fastest stamp mode and avoids boxing in runtime join state.

    • capacity

      public JoinSpec<O> capacity(int capacity)
      Returns a copy with a different maximum number of open join groups.

    • timeout

      public JoinSpec<O> timeout(Duration timeout)
      Returns a copy with a different timeout for incomplete groups.

    • missingBranches

      public JoinSpec<O> missingBranches(JoinSpec.MissingBranchPolicy policy)
      Returns a copy with the policy used when an input branch closes or times out before a group is complete.

    • duplicates

      public JoinSpec<O> duplicates(JoinSpec.DuplicatePolicy policy)
      Returns a copy with the policy used when a branch supplies the same stamp more than once.

    • kind

      public JoinSpec.JoinKind kind()
      Returns whether this join waits for all branches or emits on any branch.

    • combiner

      public Function<JoinGroup,? extends O> combiner()
      Returns the combiner invoked for completed or policy-emitted groups.

    • stampExtractor

      public Function<Object,?> stampExtractor()
      Returns an object stamp extractor.

      For long-stamped specs this adapter boxes the primitive long stamp; prefer extractLongStamp(Object) when longStamp() is true.

    • longStamp

      public boolean longStamp()
      Returns true when this spec uses primitive long stamps.

      Long stamps avoid boxing in the runtime join table. The default allOf(Function) and anyOf(Function) specs use this mode and expect Stamped inputs unless stampLong(ToLongFunction) is configured.

    • extractStamp

      public Object extractStamp(Object item)
      Extracts this item's stamp using the configured extractor.

    • extractLongStamp

      public long extractLongStamp(Object item)
      Extracts this item's primitive long stamp.
      Throws:
      IllegalStateException - if this spec uses object-valued stamps

    • capacity

      public int capacity()
      Returns the maximum number of open groups retained by this join.

    • timeout

      public Duration timeout()
      Returns the join timeout, or Duration.ZERO when disabled.

    • missingBranchPolicy

      public JoinSpec.MissingBranchPolicy missingBranchPolicy()
      Returns how incomplete groups are handled.

    • duplicatePolicy

      public JoinSpec.DuplicatePolicy duplicatePolicy()
      Returns how duplicate branch values are handled.
